steps.title: Find the key signal in the text
steps.content: The researchers want to know whether biological traits are associated with "considerable variations in venom potency." The phrase "considerable variations" signals the scale of effect being measured — something large and meaningful, not trivial or unnoticeable.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'a disconcerting': Means unsettling or disturbing — an emotional quality. The text is about measuring magnitude of variation, not how the researchers feel about the effect. ✗
  • Choice B — 'an acceptable': Means satisfactory or adequate. The text never asks whether the effect is good enough; it asks whether it is large. ✗
  • Choice C — 'an imperceptible': Means unnoticeable or too small to detect, which contradicts the goal of finding "considerable variations." ✗
  • Choice D — 'a substantial': Means sizable and significant, directly matching "considerable variations in venom potency." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
